observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly  to  the  provisions
of the Memorandum Circular No. 24 series of 2020 and of  Section  4  (3)  of
P.D. No. 1445 on  the  utilization  of  Trust  Fund.  Henceforth,  stop  the
practice of paying  various  expenditures  which  are  not  related  to  the
purpose of the funds received as required under existing regulations.

We recommended that Management:

     a. Require the City Accountant to recognize and measure the impairment
        loss in accordance with IPSAS 21 by preparing the following entry:


           Impairment Loss                                    xxx
                  Allowance for Impairment                          xxx

     b. Make a request for the write-off of the  dormant  accounts,  except
        for the  account  that  is  under  litigation,  by  processing  the
        required documents and following the procedures provided under  COA
        Circular 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed that upon the  completion  of  physical
count of the Inventory Committee, the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the
General Services Office  to  institute  measures  to  properly  dispose  all
obsolete and unserviceable properties and to require the Office of the  City
Accountant to provide impairment losses pending disposal.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the  provisions  of  COA
Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445 on the  granting,  utilization
and liquidation of  cash  advances  by  strengthening  control  measures  to
ensure timely submission of  liquidation  documents.   Furthermore,  require
the City Accountant to  issue  Demand  Letters  to  accountable  persons  to
enforce immediate settlement or otherwise impose sanctions provided  in CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 12, series 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

     a) Verify the actual ages  of  the  unliquidated  fund  transfers  and
        request for authority  to  write-off  if  the  ages  of  such  fund
        transfers qualify  them  as  dormant  unliquidated  fund  transfers
        pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;


     b) Send demand letters to concerned NGOs/POs for liquidation; and


     c) Disqualify the concerned NGOs/POs with unliquidated fund  transfers
        for new fund transfers until  their  previous  fund  transfers  are
        liquidated pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October  25,
        2007.

We recommended that Management:


      a. Direct the City Accountant and City  Treasurer  to  facilitate  the
      timely release of barangay shares and  to  return  excess  balances
      to  the  source  agencies;  and


      b. Require concerned officials to submit the  liquidation  reports  of
      the provincial aids, otherwise, return the unutilized amount to  the
      source agencies.

We recommended that Management require the proper liquidation of the  amount
granted as well as the refund of excess  project  funds  to  the  donor  and
ascertain that liquidation reports are  submitted  to  the  source  agencies
because these funds were taken up as receivables of these agencies.

We recommended that the City Government maximize the utilization of the  20%
Development Fund for programs/projects/activities by  timely  implementation
of the projects as contained in the annual Development Fund and  see  to  it
that  all  development  projects  shall  contribute  to  the  attainment  of
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the  City
and shall partake the nature of investment or capital  expenditures  as  one
of the general  policies  provided  under  DILG-DBM  JMC  No.  2020-1  dated
November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  GAD  Focal  Person
to facilitate the gathering of data/information needed for the  creation  of
a well-established GAD database to have an accurate and  reliable  basis  in
the planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the  policy  formulation,
in order to properly address  the  existing  and  emerging  GAD  issues  and
concerns.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided
under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate  compliance  of
the audit suspensions and the refund/settlement  of  the  disallowances  and
charges as provided in the RRSA. Moreover, avail of all  possible  means  as
provided by law to effect collection from those who  are  already  separated
from government service, and those who  are  not  connected  with  the  City
Government, otherwise  other  persons  determined  to  be  liable  shall  be
required to settle the same.

We recommended that the Management:
Require theCityTreasurer to send written notices to the payees of the existence of the checks and inform the payees at least one month before a check becomes stale.
Require the City Accountant to restore the liability to creditors by preparing the following adjusting entry:
Cash in Bank-LCCA                             ₱190,185.01
Accounts Payable 	 				  ₱190,185.01
To restore the payable account for stale checks

We recommended that Management strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of long outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds. Furthermore, require the City Accountant to issue Demand Letters to accountable persons to enforce immediate settlement or otherwise impose sanctions provided in COA Circular 2012-04.  Finally, comply with the requirements and settle the deficiencies noted as stipulated in the AOMs from ICFAU, COA Central Office.
We recommended that Management file malversation cases against erring accountable officers and monitor the recovery of the cash shortages.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to record the liquidations made in accordance with COA Circular 94-013. Further, prepare adjusting entry as follows:
Donations (current year grant)		₱539,357.54
Government Equity (prior year grants)	₱1,065,002.00
Due from LGUs 				₱1,604,357.54
To take up liquidation of financial assistance to barangays

We recommended that the City Accountant investigate and ascertain the causes of non-existing and unaccounted inventories then make the proper adjustment thereof and the Accountant and Property Officer conduct reconciliation of inventory records on a periodic basis.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to trace all the necessary payments of the projects with unrepaid amount of the advance payments granted by the agency and determine status of project completion and payments, otherwise the terms of the security submitted by the contractors should be enforced.
We also recommended that Management instruct the City Engineer to require contractors of supervised infrastructure project to submit progress billings of work accomplished or completed to facilitate the recoupment of advance payments made, otherwise the concerned project engineer shall be held liable for any balance of unpaid advances with expired security guarantees.

We recommended that management, thru the City Assessor’s Office and the Property Unit of the General Services Office, convert the manual certificate of titles to eTitles to safeguard its proof of ownership of its assets and avoid a tedious and expensive reconstitution of lost or destroyed certificates of land title.

We recommended that management, thru the City Assessor’s Office and the Property Unit of the General Services Office, exert further efforts to locate the documents establishing the legality of ownership of the subject lots thru proper coordination with the heads of concerned departments, and exhaust all possible means to register / transfer ownership under the Torrens Title System.
We further recommended that should the missing owner’s OCTs / TCTs be established lost for all legal intents and purposes, management immediately institute the necessary proceedings in court to secure the re-issuance thereof.

We recommended that upon the completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommended that Management instruct:
a. 	the OIC-City Engineer issue Certificates of Completion of these projects to be used as basis by the City Accountant in the recording and reclassification to the proper PPE account;
b. 	the City Accountant take up the corresponding depreciation expenses of these projects from the time these were completed until December 31, 2019; and
c. 	henceforth, classify completed projects to the proper PPE accounts to present these fairly in the financial statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the General Services Office remind the accountable officers of the responsibility and steps in requesting for relief from property accountability to be submitted to COA pursuant to Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular 92-386, which will serve as basis for the dropping of lost and/or destroyed properties from the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Management require theCityEngineer’s Office to exercise extra care and diligence in the preparation of program of work for infrastructure projects to ensure that determination of quantities and cost estimates are in accordance with Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to arrive at the most equitable Approved Budget for the Contract to be bid and implemented.  If due to unforeseen causes, changes to the original plans/contracts are required, management should immediately copy furnish COA with the variation/change order for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Management require theCityEngineer’s Office to comply with DPWH Order No. 197 series of 2016 in the preparation of the approved budget for the contract to avoid excessive amount of contract costs.

We recommended that Management:
a. 	Direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to facilitate the release of barangay shares and to return excess balances to the source agencies; and
b. 	Require concerned officials to revisit the memorandum of agreements/ undertakings for the realization of unimplemented projects, otherwise, return the unutilized amount to the source agencies.

We recommended that Management require the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor; ascertain that liquidation reports were submitted to the source agencies because these funds were taken up as receivables from these agencies.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to prepare the PMR to be approved by the HOPE and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRRs of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that management submit within 15 days from receipt of the audit observation memorandum the 210 missing checks/vouchers and supporting documents, otherwise, non-submission of these required documents may cause the disallowance of the transactions.

We recommended that management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents

We recommended that the City Treasurer provide the final computation of RPT/SET, including penalties on unpaid real property, and demand immediate payment of the same in order that the government may not be deprived of additional revenues to finance other developmental projects   Further, initiate a demand from Belian Inns and Hotel Inc. for payment of their business taxes.
Otherwise, the City Government may avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property and sale of real property by public auction or by judicial action.
We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prepare the financial statements of the Tagbilaran City College, an economic enterprise, and consolidate these in the General Fund.

We recommended that Management make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit through compliance with the requirements for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.

We recommended that the City Government maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for programs/projects/activities as contained in the annual Development Fund and see to it that all development projects shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the City and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures as one of the general policies provided under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City’s GFPS to lead or spearhead the setting up and maintenance of a GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in compliance with the undated PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the end of January to facilitate timely monitoring and evaluation and determine whether the activities based on the DILG approved GAD Plan and Budget were implemented.

We recommended that management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with audit suspensions and the refund/settlement of the disallowances and charges as provided in the RRSA. Moreover, avail of all possible means as provided by law to effect collection to those who are already separated from government service, and those who are not connected with the City Government, otherwise other persons determined to be liable shall be required to settle the same.

We recommended that management instruct the Accounting Processor to remit immediately taxes due to BIR, otherwise, penalties on late remittances should be her personal liability in accordance with Section 251 of the NIRC and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98. All taxes withheld should be remitted within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made.

We recommended that an explanation be given why the construction of work was totally suspended for a period of 203 days despite the absence of a valid reason for allowing so, and why it took that long for the City Government to secure a suitable relocation site for the vendors. We likewise recommended that the concerned officials in governing the city’s infrastructure projects, minimize and manage, if not completely avoid, work suspensions, unless really warranted under prevailing circumstances such as those authorized under Item 9 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 as afore-cited.
observations.managementAction: The rehabilitation of the Cogon Public Market is still on-going. As of the moment, it is now already working on its drainage system of which variation order is being awaited in order to continue the construction works.
observations.reason: This project was under the former CEO Department Head who has already retired. Thus, explanation for a valid reason for the suspension of work can be thoroughly answered by the former head of office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to terminate the project pursuant to Item 3.2, Annex D, R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and, if warranted and after due process, consider the contractor for blacklisting.
observations.managementAction: The awarded contractor, Maconark Builders, for the construction of Tagbilaran City Welcome Landmarks has issued a justification letter as to why the constructions have not yet been started. COA has been furnished with this justification letter last January 20, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management shall take appropriate action to pinpoint responsibility on the spoiled food commodities and to require refund for the cost thereof. Likewise, Management shall show proof that the unaccounted goods totaling P1,332,646.20 were properly distributed to the beneficiaries of the program, otherwise, this amount will not be allowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: This finding happened during the past administration and can no longer be tolerated by the current administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Assessor to effect the general revision of real property assessments immediately and every three (3) years thereafter in order to increase real property tax income that could be spent for priority projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: Formally requested assistance from the members of the Technical Working Group – Department of Finance, Engr. Dizon – Provincial Assessor of Pampanga, and Engr. Gilbas- Asst. Provincial Assessor of Sorsogon to study and assist in coming up with a more realistic Schedule of Market Values.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Sangguniang Panlungsod enact an ordinance to stop and correct the effectivity of the Ordinance No. IIC-003 dated September 7, 2012 considering that it is legally applicable to the period 2013 only as provided in Article 282 (iv).
observations.managementAction: Had been taken for deliberation in the Sanggunian Panlungsod (SP). However, majority members of the SP are inclined not to enact an ordinance to correct the effectivity of the previous ordinance being questioned. Documents related to this is submitted to COA Central Office Legal Department for their appropriate action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer provides the final computation of RPT/SET including penalties on unpaid real property for the periods CY 2005 to 2012 and 2014 to present and demand immediate payment of the same in order that the government may not be deprived of additional revenues to finance other developmental projects. Also demand Gross Income Statement during these periods from the establishment for the computation and payment of business taxes. Otherwise, the City Government may avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property and sale of real property by public auction or by judicial action.
observations.managementAction: As per AAPSI for CY 2016 dated August 25, 2016, billing statement was sent to ASC-ICM. However, as per his letter dated March 22, 2017, the City Treasurer stated that they did not proceed in sending RPT billings to ASC-ICM due to some legal issues that had come up and until now not yet resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the CSWMO In-Charge to properly monitor the actual accomplishments of the Swampy Bulldozer Operator at the dumpsite and to submit explanation on the accomplishment made which are inconsistent with the actual services rendered. We further recommended that a person will be assigned to monitor the work done by the Swampy Operator and actual accomplishment will be recorded thereon. We also recommended that instead of renting a bulldozer, management appropriate amount for the purchase of a bulldozer for the Dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: This happened during the past administration. It has been practiced. The rental payments were still made by the past administration. The current City Administration will require the CSWMO In-Charge to properly monitor the actual accomplishments. The practice is being reviewed. There is an ongoing review. Rental of swampy bulldozer is still being practiced but the number of rentals and the monitoring are being improved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the City Treasurer to send written
notices to the payees of the
existence of the checks and inform the payees at least one (1)
month before a check becomes stale.
b. Require the City Accountant to debit Cash in Bank-Local Currency,
Current Account and credit Accounts Payable for ₱143,198.34 to
restore the liability to creditors.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the CAO and GSO to analyze and reconcile regularly the PPE
records and accounts. Further the CAO has to prepare the necessary
journal entries to reclassify the duly verified unserviceable properties to
appropriate asset account awaiting for disposal in order to reflect the
correct account balances.
b. Instruct the Head of the Disposal Committee to initiate the process of
disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of
PD 1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-
386.
c. After the disposal, require the Disposal Committee to submit the
necessary documents together with the Official Receipt to the Accounting
Office, as basis for the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from
the book of accounts in accordance with existing accounting and auditing
rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the General Services Officer to maintain an efficient property
management by following the prescribed procedures in property
recording and maintain accurate property records and regularly renew
PAR;
b. Require Accounting Office to maintain an updated PPE ledger cards
reconciled with the property cards of the GSO. Section 79 of PD 1445 and
the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to:
a. Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all
reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
b. Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and
effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and
substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted
reconciling items.

We recommended that Management require the concerned barangay
officials to liquidate all financial assistance and refrain from granting additional
financial assistance to barangays particularly those with substantial unliquidated
balances.

We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to trace all
the necessary payments of the projects with unrepaid amount of the advance
payments granted by the agency and determine status of project completion and
payments, otherwise the terms of the security submitted by the contractors
should be enforced.

We also recommended that Management instruct City Engineer to require
contractors of supervised infrastructure project to submit progress billings of
work accomplished or completed to facilitate the recoupment of advance
payments made, otherwise the concerned project engineer shall be held
responsible for any balance of unpaid advances with expired security guarantees.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to
extra effort to determine the composition of the Payable accounts totaling
₱4,648,165.78 in order to ascertain its validity and to effect remittance or
payments thereof.

We recommended the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) to strictly
observe the period in the submission of copies of perfected contracts including
purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Audit Team as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Initiate the immediate settlement of ₱577,776.50 representing the
aggregate difference of the amount paid based on work accomplishments
in SWA as against the amount for the actual accomplishments per COA
Technical Inspection; and
b. Require the Inspection Team and the City Engineer to explain their
inability to note the difference of the actual accomplishment as against
the SWA that caused the overpayment as to project accomplishment.

We recommended that management submit within 15 days from receipt
hereof the 3,723 missing checks/vouchers and supporting documents, otherwise,
non-submission of these required documents may cause the disallowance of the
transactions.

We recommended that management appropriate sufficient fund to
implement properly the Solid Waste Management including the provision of
personal protective equipment for use of the garbage collectors and ensure that
same is included in the Annual Procurement Plan.

We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of
development projects upon availability of funds and ensure that the allocated funds
are timely and optimally utilized for projects identified in accordance with the
guidelines prescribed under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

We recommended that Management maximize the use of LDRRMF and
implement programmed projects in the LDRRMFIP to efficiently manage and
address eventualities for risk or emergencies due to occurrence of man-made -or
natural calamities.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) furnish the
Audit Team a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report for proper evaluation.
Thereafter, ensure that a copy corresponding Accomplishment Report be
submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from the end of January of the
preceding year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided
under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the
audit suspensions and the refund/settlement of the disallowances and charges as
provided in the RRSA.

Require the accountant to recognize the interest income earned and henceforth regularly monitor all savings and time deposits so that all interest income earned will be recognized in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: P672,056.19 has already been recognized and recorded by the Office of the City Accountant (OCA) for the period stated. Furthermore, the interest income reflected in the financial statements as of June 30, 2018 was already accrued in accordance to PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the finding of a suitable location and allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of its own sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within specific time frame.
observations.managementAction: The closure on the operation of an open dumpsite and the establishment of environmentally acceptable controlled dump facilities for the sustained preservation and protection of the environment and people’s health such as the sorting building for waste disposal and segregation or the Residual Containment Area (RCA) were being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-City Planning and Development Coordinator, City Engineer and City BAC be required to facilitate the implementation of these projects as well as other development projects of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: As of present, most of these projects were already completed. The reasons behind the delay in its implementation were given justifiable explanation. Hence, the recommendation has been well taken by the management. The responsible offices that were mentioned are coordinating in order to facilitate the implementation of the development projects of the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant record the interests on the savings and time deposit accounts at gross and reflect the corresponding withholding taxes for more accurate recording and reporting of transactions or if they opt to record it at net amount, disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount of tax deducted from the interest.
observations.managementAction: The interest income reflected in the financial statements as of December 31, 2017. Currently, the Accountant records the interests on the savings and time deposit accounts at gross and reflects the corresponding withholding taxes for more accurate recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant facilitate the preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements for accounts maintained by the City with its depository banks and conduct a regular reconciliation of balances with the City Treasurer’s Office to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the Special/High Yield Savings and Time Deposit accounts as of December 31, 2017 were already submitted last April 18, 2018. Currently, the available updated BRS that has been submitted was as of the 1st Quarter of 2018. The Office of the City Accountant is continuing to facilitate in the timely preparation and submission of BRS quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer Officials, together with the Local Finance Committee, revisit the collection efforts on the revenue with decreased collection and impose revenue, whenever fair and legal, from pertinent individual/establishments.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2016, there was a sudden bloat of the revenue collections of the City Government. This was because of the inclusion of arrears in the fines, penalties and charges especially those from the uncollected revenue from the past administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the completed projects amounting to P14,563,190.64 to appropriate asset accounts and set up liability for the unpaid balance in accordance with paragraphs 4 and 5, Section 50, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The completed projects have already been recorded in their proper asset account only upon final billing since this is only the time that all the documents supporting the completion of the project are intact, not on the basis of the City Engineering’s Office report of completed projects. The OCA cannot just record the asset account without the other necessary documents to substantiate the report. A disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding completed projects that are not yet settled or paid as of financial reporting date has already been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Finance Committee and the Sanggunian Panlungsod evaluate and consider implementation of Wage Order No. ROVII-20 considering the financial necessity and morale of the Job Order Personnel.
observations.managementAction: Current wage rate for Job Order (JOs) personnel has been increased. From ₱200.00 to ₱280.00 while for college graduates and licensed professionals were ₱300.00 and ₱330.00 respectively. This has been supported with a Memorandum Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC be required to submit a valid justification for not directing the bidders / suppliers to submit all the documents required in Appendix A of Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA9184, and to give details on the steps taken by the procurement officials in determining their eligibility and the legal basis for resorting to the same. We also highly recommend that the procurement officials strictly comply with the documentary requirements under existing laws and rules so as to ensure that the City Government only transacts with suppliers that are technically, legally and financially eligible to participate in the bidding.
observations.managementAction: The City BAC have already been aware of the revised IRR of R.A. 9184. Thus, the documentary requirements for the procurement were already updated in accordance to the revisions and were being required by the BAC to determine that the bidders are technically, legally and financially eligible to participate in the bidding. Furthermore, the documentary requirements of the winning bidder will also be checked as to its completeness and compliance upon pre-audit on the Office of the City Accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government, thru its concerned officials, submit a valid justification for its failure to comply with the time periods prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly, in the signing of the project contract and the issuance of the Notice to Proceed, and for failing to require strict and equal compliance thereof from the winning bidder, if the same be due to the latter’s doing. We likewise, and again, recommended that Management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its performance in terms of compliance with reportorial requirements, the delivery of basic services and the full implementation of programs, plans and activities for the optimal benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: In as much as the City Government wanted to implement all projects according to schedule, the lack of manpower of the City BAC affected the timeliness of the transactions. However, during the second semester of 2017, additional manpower and the presence of an Executive Assistant to oversee all the procurement activities will help play a vital role in minimizing or eradicating irregularities or deficiencies on our procurement system. With regard to the signing of the project contract, the date stated therein was not exactly the date it was signed by the approving authority but it was the date when the signed contract was received by the BAC. Thus, it also contributed to the number of days being delayed. More so, this was not given much attention during that time since there are also a lot of projects for the Bohol Earthquake Assistance (BEA) that is being processed at hand. The BAC Secretariat is now studying the process and document flow to correct deficiencies and ensure a smooth and fully personalized service. Furthermore, the BAC is also committed to comply with and give due consideration on the prescribed timelines and exert serious efforts to prevent the incurrence of similar delays in other procurement projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a legal justification be given for allowing the posting of a surety bond instead of withholding a certain amount out of the progress billing as retention money without a prior written request from the contractor. We likewise recommend that the retention money supposed to be withheld in the first progress billing be deducted in the payments that may thereafter be made to the contractor in addition to further retentions to be imposed in succeeding billings. Finally, we recommend that the requirements on retention money be strictly imposed so as to prevent any difficulty in recovering from any loss brought about by uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities.
observations.managementAction: The OCA Pre-audit division has now come up with a Pre-Audit Checklist that has been already followed and implemented to all offices to comply with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer and the City Engineer require the contractor, LABB Construction, to submit further justification of the failure to start the projects at their effectivity date, failure to comply the requirement of the Notice to Proceed and for not posting a project information the projects sites.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2016, the City Engineer submitted a justification letter to COA, LABB Construction, has been issued dated October 2, 2017 and it was indorsed by the City Engineer. It has been given explanation regarding the absence of billboard and late start of the projects was due to the traffic congestion, re-routing and on-going construction at Miguel Parras Street.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately the AAPSI for CY 2016 AAR. Henceforth, please require submission of AAPSI and post in the agency’s website within sixty (60) days from receipt of the CY 2017 AAR in compliance with Section 93 of the General Provisions of CY 2018 GAA, requiring the same as that of Section 88 of CY 2017 GAA.
observations.managementAction: With the hope of placing a position for internal auditor/s and establishing an Internal Audit Services/Unit under the City Mayor’s Office, these matters of concern would be given further attention. Rest assured, the submission of AAPSI for CY 2017, including the Status of Implementation for CY 2016 AAR as being reflected in the AAPSI report for prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point to take the lead in the preparation of GAD plans, programs, projects, and activities including its budget and to monitor its implementation and report on its results. Henceforth, we recommended that the City strictly observe the provisions set forth under DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004, including the preparation and implementation of the GAD Plans, Budget and Accomplishment report.
observations.managementAction: The management has well taken the recommendation regarding timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and Report on Fund Utilization and Accomplishment Report and to take note of the deadlines to avoid the same scenario. Moreover, it was their regret for not being able to submit these reports for CY 2017 within five (5) days after the endorsement. The GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for CY 2018 of LGU Tagbilaran was reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Director last September 26, 2017. Furthermore, the GAD reports were also posted in the Full Disclosure Policy and the LGU Tagbilaran’s website. Allocation for GAD pursuant to existing rules and regulations is at least 5% of the annual budget, thus for CY 2018, it has budgeted an amount of ₱52,300,000.00 which is 5% of the LGU- City’s annual budget amounting to ₱1,046,000,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, Management require the OIC-City Accountant to observe BIR rules on the withholding of professional taxes and any forms of taxes due to the government as required by revenue regulations.
observations.managementAction: Withholding of professional taxes and any forms of taxes due to BIR were already done by the Office of the City Accounting. The pre-audit division does the computation in withholding the tax to be deducted and the assigned personnel for BIR taxes and remittances is going to review the computation as well before recording and remitting it to BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Assessor’s Office, GSO, the Office of the City Accountant and other concerned offices to coordinate with each other in the identification of the underlying transactions involving the questioned parcels of land, and exert further efforts in locating relevant documents that would support their recording in the books of accounts. We also recommended that for those assets donated without value, Management create a committee that will appraise their values for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: As of date, the GSO Inventory & Property Division was able to locate relevant documents that were made as supporting such as the copies of Tax Declaration and the corresponding location map. They were able to forward these to the Office of the City Accountant (OCA) to effect the proper recording of land and road network accounts in the books of Government. Among the total of 601 lots being reflected in COA findings, it has been noted that there should only be a total of 599 lots with a total lot area of 567,520.16 sq. m. This is because title no. 62203 located at Dao District and title no. 59410 located at San Isidro District were repeatedly listed in the given list of titles for appraisal by the CGSO upon tallied by COA in its findings. Thus, out of these 599 lots, a total of 479 lots with a total area of 364,141 sq. m. were retrieved and appraised. These lots were already recorded and taken up in the books amounting to ₱25,882,340.00 in total.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the City Assessor’s Office and the Property Unit of the GSO, exert further efforts to locate the documents establishing the land legality of ownership of the subject lots thru proper coordination with the heads of concerned departments, and exhaust all possible means to register/transfer under the name of the City Government. We further recommended that should the missing owner’s OCTs / TCTs be established lost for all legal intents and purposes, Management immediately institute the necessary proceedings in court to secure the re-issuance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Registry of Deeds (ROD) – Bohol has a difficulty in retrieving some of the untitled city lots due to lack of some details (e.g. Lot Number) that were only available in the GSO Inventory List. Furthermore, the City Assessor’s Office (CAssO) also has a difficulty in registering/transferring the lots since the necessary deeds of conveyance and other related papers were lost and if ever, there is a remote possibility of its recovery, the office is undermanned and under time constraints to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue its efforts to at least reduce to if not zero out, the enduring variance in the PPE accounts thru constant reconciliation and coordination between the City Assessor’s Office, Office of the City Account and the Property Unit of the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices are constantly coordinating with regard to the reconciliation of records, however, it is yet to be discussed and is also being agreed for both offices if the OCA should affix its signature in the Inventory Report in order to verify the balances stated therein to tally with the City Government’s books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the City Accountant to send confirmation letters to the identified debtors and demand payment thereof. b. Request authority to write-off, those receivables of very remote collectability, from this Commission, attaching to said request all the documentary evidences proving that all means and efforts have been exhausted to collect them. This includes the issuance of demand letters as proof of exhaustion of all remedies in collecting the receivables and demanding to liquidate the cash advances and the fund transfers, efforts of locating the responsible officials, collection of death certificates for deceased accountable officer/employee/debtor, and the other justifications that is to be reflected in the request to write-off. c. Require the City Accountant to enforce the submission of the required liquidation documents for the financial assistance granted to the concerned barangays/ NGS/POs where the intended projects/activities have already been implemented in order to reflect the true and fair presentation of the account Due from LGUs/NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant (OCA) is making efforts to have these balances reduce in compliance with COA Circular 2016-005, the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), LGUs and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs). Schedule of Dormant Accounts which remained non-moving for ten (10) years or more is being prepared in order to support the request to write-off the dormant accounts. Moreover, the OCA is also strictly observing the implementation of “No Liquidation, No Cash Advance” policy in order to reduce incidence of long outstanding unliquidated Cash Advances. As of July 31, 2018, the balance of ₱25,756,604.24 was reduced to ₱25,401,059.54. The Trust Fund had a reduction of ₱302,180.00 which now gives a total of ₱14,037,242.27 that is still outstanding for over 360 days. While the General Fund had a reduction of ₱53,364.70 which now gives a total of ₱11,363,817.27 for the outstanding receivables for 700 days and over.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Conduct regular monitoring and assessment of the targets and strategies as stated in the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) to ensure the efficient management of solid wastes; 2. Enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure efficient compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the City was already submitted and approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission last CY 2017. Moreover, the implementation of the SWM Plan is already in progress. Closure on the operation of open dumpsite and the establishment of environmentally acceptable controlled dump facilities for the sustained preservation and protection of the environment and people’s health such as the sorting building for waste disposal and segregation or Residual Containment Area (RCA) were being implemented. There are also on-going trainings being conducted for the SWM Barangay Committees, Coordinators and Enforcers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Submit explanation on their failure to implement projects to be funded from the SEF and TF, thus benefits were not delivered to intended beneficiaries. 2. Facilitate the formulation of definite plans for the benefit of its intended beneficiaries and hasten the implementation of the programs/projects/activities for which these funds were created. 3. Transfer back the savings/time deposits under the Special Education and Trust Funds to its respective current account.
observations.managementAction: The Department of Education has already identified projects being prescribed by the Local School Board. As a matter of fact, various Purchase Requests have already been made and funded out of our SEF as approved by the LCE. Moreover, the said projects were already earmarked by the City Budget Office and are waiting for the bidding of the different projects and the NTP being given to the winning bidder. For the year, the SEF and TF is still deposited in Special/High Yield Savings and Time deposits awaiting for implementation coming from the Local School Board. The initiative of placing these accounts on deposit is part of the financial management and strategy to seek for ways in generating income without hampering the City’s programs and projects to its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and City Treasurer reconcile the cashbook and general ledger balances at least quarterly to ensure accuracy of the recorded transactions and the reliability of the Cash-Local Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Treasurer’s Office are in close coordination on this matter. This is also to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the recorded transactions for the Cash-Local Treasury balance. The reconciliation between the Cash-Local Treasury balance per cashbook as against balance per General Ledger is still on-going. As of the moment, the City Accountant is now already in the reconciliation for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the City Accountant to verify proof of claims of the APs to have these paid. Those that have no proof of claims need to be confirmed from the debtors for them to show that the city has indebtedness to them. b. Require the City Accountant to effect liquidation of completed projects funded out of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs and return the expended amount to the funding agency.
observations.managementAction: The payable accounts which remained to be unclaimed for two (2) years as of December 31, 2017 were already closed amounting to ₱70,165,676.20. With regards to the remaining unclosed balances of this account, the management will be conducting a thorough review of these payables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Direct the City Accountant to demand immediate liquidation of funds transferred to the concerned barangays and render report on compliance thereof by the latter to the City Mayor and the appropriate legislative committee for the adoption of appropriate measures to protect the interest of the City Government; 2. Strictly follow the guidelines stated under COA Circular 2012-001 in relation to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of funds transferred to implementing agencies.
observations.managementAction: The management already started doing an analysis of this account and it was found out that the account existed before 2013. Among the total identified fund transfers, it has been noted that the Due from LGU account in the Trust Fund amounted to ₱2,075,966.19 with a reduction of ₱1,006,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Social Welfare Officer revisit its current year’s operational procedures and devise a more efficient process that will include the conduct of quarterly or even monthly re-assessments of beneficiaries to ensure that the list of eligible senior citizens which will support the release of cash advance be already updated and final, thus facilitating complete payout and eventual liquidation in the shortest possible time.
observations.managementAction: The number of days with delays for the liquidation of Cash Advances ranges from 37 to 70 days from last findings. Thus, it can be said that management is making an effort to lessen the delay in the distribution of benefits to intended beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Local School Board to include the Early Childhood Care and Development programs in the formulation of SEF Budget in the ensuing year to promote optimum growth and development of our young children and families.
observations.managementAction: The City Government will strive to incorporate the Early Childhood Care and Development programs in the SEF Budget once the priority projects are completed. The more urgent priority of the City Government is the construction of additional classrooms to accommodate the K to 12 program of DepEd.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government maximize the utilization of the transferred funds by implementing the approved projects, and properly liquidate the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor, if so provided in the terms of the grant.
observations.managementAction: There has been an amount of total fund transfer ₱1,930,258.35 that has received, been disbursed and ₱12,904,780.45 or 50.02% was not yet utilized or implemented. A total of ₱12,895,219.55 has been fully utilized and liquidated as of present. Furthermore, the excess amount of ₱20,950.54 has been made with a supplemental proposal for the upgrading of the Paglaum Center. It has been specifically requested to finance other lacking facility like electrical wiring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government implement the projects/programs/activities funded from trust funds as required in the Memorandum of Agreement or Special Allotment Advices so that the purpose for which these funds were granted are achieved, thus benefitting its constituents. Further, we recommended that any unused/unexpended balance of completed projects/programs/activities be returned to the Source Agency in compliance with the requirement under COA Circular No. 94-013. We also recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, immediately make use of the funds by planning out projects and activities of the fund after given permission from the donor/funding agency for them to utilize the fund for purposes other than what it originally intended.
observations.managementAction: The BEA Fund for the Rehabilitation of Bool Barangay Hall and Poblacion 2 Barangay Hall which has been fully utilized and liquidated. While, the allotted for the Ubujan Health Station and Integrated Bus Terminal constructions were still the same and intact. The Manga Brgy. Hall, City Vet. Bldg. And Integrated Bus Terminal constructions were not yet fully implemented and liquidated. All of their allotted balance from the Bohol Earthquake Assistance Fund were still intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CDRRM Council constantly review its Annual Investment Plan to ensure that the same not only covers all thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management, but at the same time, gives due priority to identified weak points in the City Government’s capacity to anticipate, respond to, and recover from the adverse impacts of likely, imminent or current hazard events or conditions.
observations.managementAction: The construction of Evacuation Center located in Brgy. Dampas near IBT is now ready to be used for emergencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a valid justification for its failure to act on previous audits’ recommendation urging appointment to its vacant positions. We likewise, and again, recommended that Management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its performance in terms of compliance with reportorial requirements, the delivery of basic services and the full implementation of programs, plans and activities for the optimal benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: There are already 7 mandatory positions for the LDRRMO that were currently being filled up. Moreover, another 3 positions were additionally created for 2018 that also were still unfilled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order employees. We further recommended that the hiring of job order personnel be resorted to based on necessity of their services with the goal of delivering efficient basic services due to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Additional items for regularization of employees were lobbied to be filled up before the election ban. The number of currently hired JO employees was lowered to 1,257.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management remit promptly and intact all taxes withheld to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance with the BIR regulations to avoid penalties.
observations.managementAction: Prompt remittance of the taxes withheld to the BIR is being observed by the designated personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the responsible officials/employees to remit immediately the withheld funds to GSIS. Likewise, require the Accounting Office to reconcile the accounts with abnormal balances and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance in the balance sheet.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant is religiously remitting premiums/contributions. The accounts reflected in the books are inherited balances from other personnel handling the books of accounts. Some of these were already reconciled and adjusted. The abnormal debit balances incurred from CY 2017 in the amount of ₱55,668.91 was now reduced to ₱48,633.11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the audit suspensions and the refund/settlement of the disallowances and charges as provided in the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: An internal auditor has been appointed last October in order to monitor these audit suspensions, disallowances and charges to follow up the concerned officers and employees in order for it to be complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Human Resource and Management Officer be required to submit the aforementioned requirements and provide legal basis for hiring the 8 consultants paid higher rates.
observations.managementAction: Presently, there are already no more hiring of consultants under bidding/procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation for indicating effectivity date in the NTP that is more than seven (7) days from its issuance which is not in accordance with Section 37.4.1of the R-IRR of RA 9184. We recommended that the Head, City BAC Secretariat submit copy of the posting of the NTP in PhilGEPS and the City website fifteen (15) days from its issuance. We recommended management to require the City Accountant to deduct from the payment to the supplier the liquidated damages to be imposed on late delivery and installation of supplies for traffic light and CCTV system.
observations.managementAction: The pre-audit section of the OCA has forwarded the pertaining documents in this transaction together with its suspension order for this transaction to be resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Engineer and the City Planning and Development Coordinator to submit justification on their failure to comply with the provision of Item VI.A.3.e of the DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 68 Series of 2014 dated June 3, 2014 to issue a Warning Letter to the Contractor and require the submission of a Catch-up Plan to complete the works within the approved contract period. We further recommended the CEO to submit SWA at target completion date and at date of actual completion. Furthermore, we recommended that the pre-audit section of the City Accounting Office impose liquidated damages, if circumstances warrant, based on the SWAs submitted by the CEO.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO and CEO have submitted the Statement of Work Accomplishment (SWA) which have already been submitted. Furthermore, the pre-audit section of the OCA already imposes liquidated damages to contractors for delayed implementations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a.) Confer with the National Solid Waste Management Commission regarding the approval of the submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that such plan could be implemented. b.) Give primary attention in the establishment of projects and activities for safe and sanitary management of solid waste including the conduct of information dissemination that will educate the constituents of the appropriate practices in the handling of wastes, sorting, segregation, composting, recycling and appropriate sufficient funds for the said purpose. c.) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the City was already submitted and approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission last CY 2017. Moreover, the implementation of the SWM Plan is already in progress. Closure on the operation of open dumpsite and the establishment of environmentally acceptable controlled dump facilities for the sustained preservation and protection of the environment and people’s health such as the sorting building for waste disposal and segregation or Residual Containment Area (RCA) were being implemented. There are also on-going trainings being conducted for the SWM Barangay Committees, Coordinators and Enforcers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Circular 001-2015. We further recommended that Management require the OIC-City Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institution (GFIs).
observations.managementAction: Management thru the OIC-City Treasurer has already requested the approval from the BLGF. It has also been explained that the maintaining of accounts with banks other than government financial institution was because there is a need for the current system used for the Real Property Taxes by the CTO to maintain account deposits. However, the termination of these accounts will be due the following year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management evaluate and consider implementation/increasing the daily wage rates of job order personnel.
observations.managementAction: Current wage rate for Job Order (JOs) personnel has been increased. From ₱200.00 to ₱280.00 while for college graduates and licensed professionals were ₱300.00 and ₱330.00 respectively. This has been supported with a Memorandum Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City-HRMO to strictly observe CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002, in hiring professional services under the Contract of Services, specifically the contract duration which shall not exceed six (6) months and the payment should be on a daily basis as provided under Section 1.a.2 of the said resolution.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were already complied in accordance to CSC Resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City’s Bid and Award Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) was already submitted. Consolidation for the report has already assigned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 on the monitoring and reporting of projects status and accomplishments. Submit the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented this year; notify the COA Audit Team that signboards are already posted within ten (10) days after the award of infrastructure projects or before the program/activities started; require the Officer-in-Charge of the PPAs to provide signboards in conspicuous places within the premises of the agency and at the PPA’s location; and consistently, follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 in order to attain the State’s policy of transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO submits the 2015 Status Report for the 20% Development Fund annually. On the other hand, the CEO submits quarterly or even monthly the Status Report on the implementation of projects, programs and activities to the CPDO. Thus, there has been a practice which has already been consistently implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the OIC City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of priority development projects, programs and activities during the year the funds are actually appropriated to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and desired outcomes from the programs/projects.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO already submits the Status Report for the 20% Development Fund annually, and the CEO submits quarterly or monthly the Status Report on the implementation of projects, programs and activities to the CPDO. There is close and continuous coordination among the offices involved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue to attach the picture and name of the mayor/any political personality on government programs, projects and activities (PPA) and adhere strictly to the aforecited provisions of COA Circular COA Circular 2013-004 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101.
observations.managementAction: The practice has already been stopped and these supplies were already consumed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate compliance of the full disclosure policy of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2010-083 dated August 31, 2010, as amended. Further, we recommended that management in coordination with the City Public Information Office, regularly update the postings in the designated bulletin boards in at least three (3) publicly accessible and conspicuous places in the City and also in the City’s website.
observations.managementAction: Compliance of the full disclosure policy of the DILG were already implemented. Regular updating of the postings in the designated bulletin boards to publicly accessible and conspicuous places in the City and also in the LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the List of Government Purchases/ Contracts of Services and submit the same within 15 days after the end of each quarter to the BIR in compliance with the requirements of Presidential Memorandum Order No. 219. Likewise, furnish this Office of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant diligently submits to the BIR within the prescribed period the following lists: Quarterly Alphabetical List of Payees (QAP) – on the 30th day of the month following the close of the quarter; Annual Information Return & Annual Alphabetical List of Payees: 1. BIR FORM 1604-E – on or before March 1 of the following year in which payments were made; 2. BIR FORM 1604-F – on or before January 31 of the ff. year in which payments were made; 3. BIR FORM 1604-CF – on or before January 31 of the year ff. the calendar year in which the compensation payment and other income payments subject to final withholding taxes were paid or accrued; 4. BIR FORM 2316 – on or before February 28 of the succeeding year, with accompanying Certified List of Employees Qualified for Substituted Filing of ITR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Budget Officer use the proper account classification in obligating transactions and the OIC City Accountant to record them in conformity with state accounting standards.
observations.managementAction: Recording of transactions is already done in accordance to PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Require the Gowil Builders to immediately correct the defects and to adhere strictly to the provisions of the R-IRR of RA 9184; 2. Withhold final payment unless corrections of these defects are found effected.
observations.managementAction: These road projects are from the past administration. It has been completed on the year when the earthquake struck the province. Currently, these roads were accessible to the public. Moreover, GOWIL Builders has not been a bidder anymore for the projects of the current administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Require the OIC-Accountant to comply with the provisions stated in Section 30 of the NGAS Manual Volume III in recording and reporting fund transfers to other LGUs and Barangays. 2. Monitor the utilization of the funds released for project implementation by requiring the recipients to submit liquidation reports. 3. Require the barangays listed in Annex F to submit justification for non-liquidation/utilization of funds intended for development projects.
observations.managementAction: The fund releases to barangays that were recorded directly as expenses are those that are not part of the Capital Outlay. Moreover, OCA can still have control over the monitoring of the liquidations made by the barangays since “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy is being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the OIC-Accountant to observe proper recording of transactions in accordance with Section 32 of New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs Volume III, ensure that all fund releases to NGOs/POs be supported by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for their proper utilization, implementation and liquidation pursuant to the provisions stipulated under COA Circular 2007-001.
observations.managementAction: The fund release to NGOs/POs are now properly recorded in accordance to PPSAS under the receivable account “Due from NGOs/POs” (1-03-06-030). Furthermore, fund releases to NGOs/POs are now supported by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and to comply with the provisions stipulated under COA Circular 2007-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Notices of Deliveries/Inspection and Acceptance Reports have been submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (C) Comply with the Guidelines in granting, utilization, accounting and auditing the funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by and between the LGU-Tagbilaran City and the NGO to avoid disallowance in audit. (2) Submit explanation for the release of amount despite non-compliance of COA Circular 2007-001. (B) Fund was transferred despite absence of Program of Work for the whole amount released. We recommended that Management: (C) Closely monitor and inspect the project implementation and verify the financial records and reports on the progress of the projects submitted by the NGO. (2) Submit explanation for the release of full amount despite that POW only amounts to P488,204.35. © Financial Report was not required from the NGO contrary to the requirement stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) entered between the LGU-Tagbilaran City and the NGO. We recommended that Management demand financial report from the NGO for the progress of the project implementation in compliance of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The fund release to a Non-Government Organization (NGO) is now properly recorded in accordance to PPSAS and the OCA has already requested the NGO to submit the necessary supporting documents. Continuously monitor the project. The documents are being gathered and the matter is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended recording of transactions to its proper account to fairly present the agency’s financial statements as of any given date.
observations.managementAction: Recording of transactions is already done in accordance to PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that collection data and/or the subsidiary ledger to be maintained by the accounting department for the collectors would include, aside from the collection date (date of OR) and amount, the RCR number and the date remittances were made by the collectors.
observations.managementAction: The documents were gathered and reports are prepared for submission. The Driver’s Trip Tickets were prepared. The department includes the RCR number and the date remittances were made by the collectors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Create an inventory committee as prescribed in Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386. 2. Require the committee to conduct inventory and submit inventory report to the local chief executive copy furnished the Accountant Office and the Commission On Audit. 3. Based on the inventory report, require the Accounting Office to reconcile in their record and prepare adjustment when applicable.
observations.managementAction: This has already been disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements. Furthermore, an Inventory Committee has been created. There has also been a constant coordination in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the OCA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the OIC-City Treasurer and OIC-City Accountant to reconcile the account balances per Cashbook and per General Ledger. Identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation between Cashbooks and General Ledger was made at year-end only due to time constraints and the lack of manpower. It has been reassured that with the infusion of additional accountants this year, a regular reconciliation of these records would be possible to ensure accuracy and reliability of the recorded transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the officials concerned to fast-track the provision of the much-needed assistance in order to accomplish the purposes for which the funds were granted and deliver funds immediate and optimum results to target beneficiaries of the Emergency Shelter Assistance.
observations.managementAction: Only partial of the ESA fund was utilized. Continuous disbursements are already being made as of 2018. The remaining unutilized funds for ESA is ₱2,197,040.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the mandatory positions in keeping with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121, to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: There are already 7 mandatory positions for the LDRRMO that were currently being filled up. Moreover, another 3 positions were additionally created for 2018 that also were still unfilled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit explanation on the implementation of projects not in accordance with the approved APP. Henceforth, submit APP to the office of the City COA.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Procurement Plan is now made available through the full disclosure policy of the DILG. It can also be downloadable thru the LGU website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended once again that the Local School Board (LSB) and the OIC-City Treasurer, terminate the aforementioned time/savings deposits and maximize the utilization of the fund by mapping out more projects in line with the priority needs of public schools such as repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development in accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and other related rules.
observations.managementAction: The funds were already utilized for its specific priority projects, programs and activities for the SEF as being prescribed by certain laws, rules and regulations. Moreover, the SEF was placed to this account in order to augment the income of the City while waiting for its overall implementation. This is part of the financial management and strategy to look for another way to generate income without hampering the city’s programs and projects to its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The City Accountant demand the immediate liquidation of funds transfer/financial assistance granted to various barangays for CY 2016 amounting to P4,712,272.39 by setting specific timelines for the liquidation considering that the terms of the agreement had already been violated.
observations.managementAction: The OCA is seeking ways to lessen these dormant receivables by coordinating with the incumbent barangay officials. However, the incumbent officials do not have the information/data pertaining to these fund transfers since these were made by the previous administration of the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned barangay officials to liquidate all financial assistance received especially those granted for the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into account the required documentary requirements per COA Circular No. 2009-001 and refrain from granting additional financial assistance to barangays particularly those with substantial unliquidated balances.
observations.managementAction: The OCA is seeking ways to lessen these dormant receivables by coordinating with the incumbent barangay officials. However, the incumbent officials do not have the information/data pertaining to these fund transfers since these were made by the previous administration of the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and that disbursement vouchers pertaining to payment of this transaction be supported with the following documents: a. Duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets using the prescribed form which shall be secured before undertaking any travel using government vehicles. These should be serially numbered. b. Requisition and Issue Vouchers c. Daily Gas Issue Voucher Further, require the Supply Officer or the OIC-City GSO to regularly prepare the following monthly reports to support the payment of fuel, oil and lubricants, to be submitted to the Audit Team on a periodic basis, to wit: 1. Summary of Requisition and Issue Vouchers 2. Monthly Report of Official Travels 3. Report of Fuel Consumption
observations.managementAction: The documents were gathered and reports are prepared for submission. The Driver’s Trip Tickets were prepared. The department includes the RCR number and the date remittances were made by the collectors. The pre-audit section of the City Accounting further requires duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets and the Report of Fuel Consumption monthly as supporting documents pertaining to these items. However, some offices still lacked the necessary prescribed forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Engineer’s Office to submit to COA Audit Team the suspension orders of the implementation of infrastructure projects totaling P32,363,450.21. Otherwise, require the OIC-City Accountant to impose liquidated damages to contractors to comply with Section 68 of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the documents in support of the delayed accomplishment of the contractors of various infrastructure/rehabilitation projects; prepared the suspension orders in support of the delayed payment of the contractors of various infrastructure projects. Furthermore, the pre-audit division in the Office of the City Accountant ensures that all relevant and necessary documents pertaining to disbursements are complete and certain in accordance to laws, rules and regulations. The suspension orders are attached to the delayed payment vouchers and are not furnished to COA upon its issuance to the contractor of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management designate immediately official/employee to take charge of the reconciliation of billing statements to eliminate the surcharges and to promptly pay the bills.
observations.managementAction: Only the current bills are being paid by the in-charge. With regard to the previous period pending case between the City Government and the power provider which results to accumulation of unsecured balances.
