The City Legal Office is making representation with the BLCI on the settlement of surcharges. Making representation and negotiation with the BLCI is in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Issue demand letters to all concerned parties to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately, otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees concerned for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445; 2. Request for authority to write-off unliquidated accounts for more than 10 years so that these amounts can be closed and free the books of accounts from misstated balances.
observations.managementAction: Some of these officers and employees were already retired, deceased or separated. There was already liquidation of ₱25.1M confidential expenses but no credit advice from COA. The OCA is making efforts to dig up and trace the whereabouts of these cash advances since most of these were already dormant. This is also because OCA is to comply with the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Require the concerned city department officials to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations; 2. Prepare liquidation report of those projects already completed which were funded out of the fund under the account “Due from other NGAs” and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.
observations.managementAction: Follow-ups have been made. The documents will be gathered and management is monitoring these funds to ensure utilization. The records in our books currently reflect that the “Due to Other NGAs” account in the General Fund and Trust Fund still has a balance of ₱234,162.63 and ₱94,346,415.34 respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Direct the OIC-City Treasurer to withhold the payment of any money due to Ms. Sonia G. Ingles and Mr. Lorell S. Ingles and take appropriate legal action to immediately recover the full amount. 2. Direct the OIC-City Treasurer to closely supervise the operations of the City Treasurer’s Office more particularly, in the collections, remittances, deposits and reporting of the said transactions.
observations.managementAction: Ms. Sonia Ingles was dropped from the rolls effective May 31, 2013. Meanwhile, Mr. Lorell Ingles continues to pay the unremitted collections, esp. from Ms. Sonia G. Ingles thru payroll deductions. Management has not taken any legal action to immediately recover the full amount of unremitted collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to inform the persons liable/responsible of NDs/NS issued to prepare for settlement of the audit disallowances/suspensions. Those NDs within the appealable period that amounted to P10,095,564.30 are encouraged to appeal in accordance with the provisions of the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: With the hope of establishing an Internal Audit Services/Unit under the City Mayor’s Office, these matters of concern would be closely monitored and would be given attention for immediate settlement and/or compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to enforce the submission of the required liquidation documents for the financial assistance granted to the concerned barangays where the intended projects/activities have already been implemented in order to reflect the true and fair presentation of the account Due from LGUs. We also recommended that henceforth, the Accountant should monitor the submission by the barangays concerned of the liquidation report for the financial assistance released to them.
observations.managementAction: Follow-ups have been made to barangays considering that most of these transactions were before the present barangay officials. Thus, it has been noted that the balance has lowered to ₱2,464,025.38 which were long outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against Sonia G. Ingles, Cristina N. Corloncito and all other collecting officers found to have falsified official receipts and withheld collections for their personal use as listed in the presentation of this finding and those of CY 2012 AAR.
observations.managementAction: Copies are with the Office of the City Auditor. We will be furnishing them the documents for the City Management to file appropriate action against the erring officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Direct the OIC City Accountant to include in their accounting system the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledger of Collecting Officers; 2. Direct the OIC City Accountant, with the cooperation with the OIC City Treasurer, to reconcile the records of the Accounting with that of the Collectors at least once every quarter.
observations.managementAction: There has been constant reconciliation with the Office of the Collectors. The current accounting system is not applicable to the previous recommendation as the accounting system currently being used by the OCA does not maintain the separate subsidiary ledger for each collector as a separate module.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the signatories of the documents enumerated earlier and/or the contractors or suppliers to submit justification/explanation on the deficiencies noted. 2. Require the OIC-City Accountant gather the documents, explanations as well as other reports in compliance to the suggested actions for each deficiency noted in the transactions. Subsequent transactions be supported with valid and proper documents and that deficiencies noted in this finding would not exist on those transactions.
observations.managementAction: The pre-audit division in the Office of the City Accountant ensures that all relevant and necessary documents pertaining to disbursements are complete and certain in accordance to laws, rules and regulations. Some of these documents pertaining to the payments of selected transactions in this findings were already untraceable due to the change of office location during the earthquake last 2013.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Offices of the General Services and the City Accountant to book up the appraised value of the 467 lots to reflect the actual amounts of the Land, Government Equity or Public Infrastructure accounts, including that of the remaining 45 lots as soon as the appraisal shall have been completed, and the Office of the General Services to keep a complete record of the same, including the real property card.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO and OCA are constantly coordinating for the booking up of these lots owned by the City Government. (Refer further to COA AAR 2017 Status of Implementation, AOM No. 2018-030) The recovery of the necessary supporting documents of these lots are still being worked on so that it will be booked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the BAC to follow timelines indicated in the APP of the city government.
observations.managementAction: Monitor the timelines indicated in the APP by each concerned department as BAC only accept those covered by OBR. The APP is now being implemented but some projects were still beyond the prescribed timeline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require each recipient official/employee to refund P65,000.00 and henceforth observe strictly what is allowed by law.
observations.managementAction: This is a Sangguniang Panglungsod discretion which involves legal questions. The disbursement was based on Ordinance No. 14-7 approving Supplemental Budget No. 5 series of 2014 which included the appropriation of the PEI of P70,000 for each official and employee. This ordinance likewise based on DBM Circular No. 2014-3 in relation to the Supreme Court decision in the case of Hon. RTC Judge Dadole, et.al vs. COA (G.R. No. 125350, December 3, 2002). For further review of the validity of the disbursement, the management is taking necessary action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that two of the three CEO officials be relieved from their being members of the BAC/Secretariat/TWG to give these officials time to closely supervise and monitor infrastructure projects implementation.
observations.managementAction: Their knowledge and expertise are needed. There are projects delayed because of conflict of duties in their offices and in the BAC. The BAC Secretariat is still studying the process and document flow to correct deficiencies and ensure a smooth and fully personalized service.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Confer with the National Solid Waste Commission regarding the approval of the submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that such plan could be implemented.

b.) Give primary attention in the preparation and implementation of projects and activities for safe and sanitary management of solid waste including the conduct of information dissemination that will educate the constituents of the appropriate practices in the handling of wastes, sorting, segregation, composting, or recycling and appropriate sufficient funds for the said purpose.

c.) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: The City Accountant demand the immediate liquidation of fund transfer/financial assistance granted to various barangays for CY 2016 amounting to P4,712,272.39 by setting specific timelines for the liquidation considering that the terms of the agreement had already been violated.
observations.managementAction: Incumbent officials do not have the information/data pertaining to these fund transfers since these were made by the previous administration

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management evaluate and consider implementation/increasing the daily wage rates of job order personnel.
We recommend Management to terminate the project pursuant to Item 3.2, Annex D, R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and, if warranted and after due process, consider the contractor for blacklisting.

Recommendation:
      We recommend that the City Government maintain its compliance with the
provisions of Republic Act No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular  No.  2012-
120 and continue its commitment  in  promoting  child  rights  to  survival,
development, protection, participation towards  a  friendly  Philippines,  a
caring and protective society for, by and with the children.

Recommendation:

      We recommend  that  Management  direct  the  City  Assessor’s  Office,
General Services Office,  the  Office  of  the  City  Accountant  and  other
concerned offices to coordinate with each other  in  the  identification  of
the underlying transactions involving the questioned parcels  of  land,  and
exert further efforts in locating  relevant  documents  that  would  support
their recording in the books of accounts.


      We also  recommend  that  for  those  assets  donated  without  value,
Management create a committee that will appraise  their  values  for  proper
recording.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management, thru the City Assessor’s Office and  the
Property Unit of the General  Services  Office,  exert  further  efforts  to
locate the documents establishing the legality of ownership of  the  subject
lots thru proper coordination with the heads of concerned  departments,  and
exhaust all possible means to register/transfer ownership under the  Torrens
Title System.


      We further recommend that should the missing owner’s OCTs  /  TCTs  be
established lost for all legal intents and purposes, Management  immediately
institute the necessary proceedings  in  court  to  secure  the  re-issuance
thereof.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management continue its efforts to at  least  reduce
to minimum, if not zero out, the enduring variance in the PPE accounts  thru
constant  reconciliation  and  coordination  between  the  City   Assessor’s
Office, Office of the City Account and  the  Propery  Unit  of  the  General
Services Office.

Recommendation:

      Require the accountant to recognize the  interest  income  earned  and
henceforth regularly monitor all savings  and  time  deposits  so  that  all
interest  income  earned  will  be  recognized  in  order  to  have  a  fair
presentation of the financial statements.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that Management prioritize the finding  of  a  suitable
location and allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of  its
own sanitary landfill  in accordance with Sections 40 and 41  of  R.A  9003.
In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site  into  a
controlled dump and hasten its closure within  specific time frame.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management:

     1. Conduct regular  monitoring  and  assessment  of  the  targets  and
        strategies as stated in the Solid Waste Management Plan  (SWMP)  to
        ensure the efficient management of solid wastes;


     2. Enforce strictly the  City  Ordinances  and  strategies  to  ensure
        efficient compliance thereof.

Recommendation:

    We recommend the OIC-City Planning and  Development  Coordinator,  City
Engineer and City Bids and Awards Committee be required  to  facilitate  the
implementation of these projects as well as other  development  projects  of
the City Government.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management:


     1. Submit explanation on their failure to  implement  projects  to  be
        funded from the SEF and TF, thus benefits  were  not  delivered  to
        intended beneficiaries.


     2. Facilitate the formulation of definite plans for the benefit of its
        intended  beneficiaries  and  hasten  the  implementation  of   the
        programs/projects/activities for which these funds were created.


     3. Transfer back the savings/time deposits under the Special Education
        and Trust Funds to its respective current account.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Accountant record the interests on  the  savings
and  time  deposit  accounts  at  gross  and   reflect   the   corresponding
withholding taxes for more accurate recording and reporting of  transactions
or if they opt to record  it  at  net  amount,  disclose  in  the  Notes  to
Financial Statements the amount of tax deducted from the interest.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Accountant facilitate the  preparation  and
submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements for accounts maintained by  the
City with its depository banks  and  conduct  a  regular  reconciliation  of
balances with  the  City  Treasurer’s  Office  to  ensure  completeness  and
accuracy of the recorded transactions.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Accountant and City Treasurer reconcile the
cashbook and general ledger balances at least quarterly to  ensure  accuracy
of  the  recorded  transactions  and  the  reliability  of  the   Cash-Local
Treasury.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management:

   a. Require the City Accountant to verify proof of claims of  the  APs  to
      have these paid.   Those that have no  proof  of  claims  need  to  be
      confirmed from the debtors  for  them  to  show  that  the  city  have
      indebtedness to them.


   b. Require  the  City  Accountant  to  effect  liquidation  of  completed
      projects funded out of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs and return  the
      expended amount to the funding agency.

Recommendation:


            We recommend that the City Treasurer  Officials,  together  with
the Local Finance Committee, revisit the collection efforts on  the  revenue
with decreased collection and impose revenue, whenever fair and legal,  from
pertinent individual/establishments.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management:


     1. Direct the City Accountant to demand immediate liquidation of funds
        transferred  to  the  concerned  barangays  and  render  report  on
        compliance thereof  by  the  latter  to  the  City  Mayor  and  the
        appropriate legislative committee for the adoption  of  appropriate
        measures to protect the interest of the City Government;


     2. Strictly follow the guidelines stated under COA  Circular  2012-001
        in relation to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13,  1994  on
        the grant, utilization and  liquidation  of  funds  transferred  to
        implementing agencies.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  that  the  City  Accountant  reclassify  the  completed
projects amounting to P14,563,190.64 to appropriate asset accounts  and  set
up liability for the unpaid balance in accordance with paragraphs 4  and  5,
Section 50, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.


      Further, validate and account for the details of the long  outstanding
CIP balance.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Social Welfare Officer revisit its  current
operational procedures  and  devise  a  more  efficient  process  that  will
include  the  conduct  of  quarterly  or  even  monthly  re-assessments   of
beneficiaries to ensure that the list  of  eligible  senior  citizens  which
will support the release of cash advance be already updated and final,  thus
facilitating complete  payout  and  eventual  liquidation  in  the  shortest
possible time.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that  the  City  Finance  Committee  and  the  Sanggunian
Panlungsod evaluate and consider implementation of Wage Order  No.  ROVII-20
considering the financial necessity and morale of the Job Order Personnel.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Management require the  Local  School  Board  to
include  the  Early  Childhood  Care  and  Development   programs   in   the
formulation of SEF Budget in the ensuing year to promote optimum growth  and
development of our young children and families.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Government maximize the utilization of  the
transferred funds by implementing the approved  planned  programs,  projects
and activities with the partner agencies so that the purpose for  which  the
funds were granted is achieved, thus benefiting constituents.


      The City should likewise implement proper liquidation  of  the  amount
granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the  donor,  if  so
provided in the terms of the grant.

Recommendation:

      We   recommend   that    the    City    Government    implement    the
projects/programs/activities funded from trust  funds  as  required  in  the
Memorandum of Agreement or Special Allotment Advices  so  that  the  purpose
for which these funds  were  granted  are  achieved,  thus  benefitting  its
constituents.


      Further, we recommend that any unused/unexpended balance of  completed
projects/programs/activities be returned to the Source Agency in  compliance
with the requirement under COA Circular No. 94-013.


      We also recommend that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Council, immediately make use of the funds  by  planning  out  projects  and
activities of the fund after given permission from the donor/funding  agency
for them to utilize the fund for purposes  other  than  what  it  originally
intended.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the  CDRRM  Council  constantly  review  its  Annual
Investment Plan to ensure that the same not only covers all  thematic  areas
of disaster risk reduction and management, but at the same time,  gives  due
priority to identified weak points in  the  City  Government’s  capacity  to
anticipate, respond to, and recover from  the  adverse  impacts  of  likely,
imminent or current hazard events or conditions.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Planning and Development  Officer  and  the
City Engineer require the contractor, LABB Construction, to  submit  further
justification of the failure to start  the  projects  at  their  effectivity
date, failure to comply the requirement of the Notice  to  Proceed  and  for
not posting a project information the projects sites.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management submit  a  valid  justification  for  its
failure to act on previous audits’ recommendation urging appointment to  its
vacant positions.

      We likewise, and  again,  recommend  that  Management  prioritize  and
consider filling-up the vacant  positions  to  enhance  its  performance  in
terms of compliance with reportorial requirements,  the  delivery  of  basic
services and the full implementation of programs, plans and  activities  for
the optimal benefit of the constituents.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  that  Management  assess  its  existing  organizational
structure  so  that  the  ideal  number  of  plantilla  personnel  will   be
established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of  job  order
employees.

      We further recommend  that  the  hiring  of  job  order  personnel  be
resorted  to  based  on  necessity  of  their  services  with  the  goal  of
delivering efficient basic services due to the constituents.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management instruct the City  Accountant  and  other
concerned officials to  submit  immediately  the  AAPSI  for  CY  2016  AAR.
Henceforth, please require submission of AAPSI  and  post  in  the  agency’s
website within  sixty  (60)  days  from  receipt  of  the  CY  2017  AAR  in
compliance with  Section 93 of  the  General  Provisions  of  CY  2018  GAA,
requiring the same as that of Section 88 of CY 2017 GAA.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal  Point  to  take
the  lead  in  the  preparation  of  GAD  plans,  programs,  projects,   and
activities including its  budget  and  to  monitor  its  implementation  and
report on its results.

      Henceforth, we recommend that the City strictly observe the provisions
set forth under DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01  dated  April
5, 2004, including the preparation and  implementation  of the  GAD  Plans,
Budget and Accomplishment report.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management  remit  promptly  and  intact  all  taxes
withheld to the Bureau of  Internal  Revenue  in  compliance  with  the  BIR
regulations to avoid penalties.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  that  henceforth  Management   require   the   OIC-City
Accountant to observe BIR rules on  the withholding  of  professional  taxes
and any forms of  taxes  due  to  the  government  as  required  by  revenue
regulations.

Recommendation:

      We    recommend    that    Management    require    the    responsible
officials/employees  to  remit  immediately  the  withheld  funds  to  GSIS.
Likewise, require the Accounting  Office  to  reconcile  the  accounts  with
abnormal balances and make the necessary adjusting entries  to  correct  the
account balance in the balance sheet.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management exhaust all available  remedies  provided
under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate  compliance  of
the audit suspensions and the refund/settlement  of  the  disallowances  and
charges as provided in the RRSA.
We recommend that the OIC-City Treasurer requires all check issuance/fund/bank account be recorded in the Check Register.
We recommend that management provide adequate safety vault for safekeeping the cash/money of the Petty Cash Custodian/Special Disbursing Officers

We recommend that management officially designate a PPE Inventory Committee and immediately schedule the conduct of a physical count of all assets to ensure a fair presentation of the PPE and Inventory accounts in the financial statements. Once the RPCPPE and RPCI are made, we recommend that the same be reconciled with the stock/property cards, ledger cards and with the control accounts.

We recommend Management to stop the payment of Transportation Allowance to those Local Officials and Employees who are assigned or who use government motor transportation for the actual performance of their duties and responsibilities as prescribed in the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to avoid disallowance in the future.
We recommend that management ensure the proper use of the Biometric Attendance Registry by appointed permanent/co-terminus employees in accordance with CSC MC No. 21, series of 1991.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Job Order personnel prepare and submit their actual accomplishment reports to support their services rendered.  We recommend also that the management avoid disbursement of funds with incomplete documentation.

Advise the City Treasurer to demand official receipts printed by the National Printing Office upon release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration fees to the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) and the Philippine Councilors League Incorporated (PCL). AT no instance shall old receipts be honored to acknowledge payments.

We recommend that the OIC-City Planning and Development Coordinator, City Engineer and the City Budget Officer be required to facilitate the implementation of these projects and be made to explain on its non-implementation at timelines set for its implementation.

We recommend that the City Government strictly observe the provisions set forth under Section 5.2 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 dated April 5, 2004, including the preparation and implementation of the GAD plans, budget and submit accomplishment report as required in the aforecited provision.

We recommend that management require the members of the Disposal Committee to evaluate the status of unserviceable properties and facilitate its immediate disposal by fast tracking the preparation of documents needed therefor in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of the Status of unserviceable properties was done during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an ad hoc team that will concentrate on the updating of property records and reconciliation of the balances with the City Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: General Services Office and City Accountant Office personnel had been working in the reconciliation of the PPE balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the ICO-City Treasurer and OIC-City Accountant to reconcile the account balances per Cashbook and per General Ledger. Identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the officials concerned to fast-track the provision of the much-needed assistance in order to accomplish the purposes for which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum results to target beneficiaries of the Emergency Shelter Assistance.
observations.managementAction: Only partial of the ESA fund was utilized during CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Human Resource and Management Officer be required to submit the aforementioned requirements and provide legal basis for hiring the 8 consultants paid higher rates.
observations.managementAction: Other documentary requirements, specially that of alternative mode of procurement, are not complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the mandatory positions in keeping with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121, to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: Only the head of the LDRRMO was designated. Other madatory positions are not yet filled-up

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit explanation on the implementation of projects not in accordance with the approved APP.  Henceforth, submit APP to the office of the City COA.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit report for action taken on the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit an explanation for indicating effectivity date in the NTP that is more than seven (7) days from its issuance which is not in accordance with Section 37.4.1of the R-IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Head, City BAC Secretariat submit copy of the posting of the NTP in PhilGEPS and the City website fifteen (15) days from its issuance.

We recommend management to require the City Accountant to deduct from the payment to the supplier the liquidated damages to be imposed on late delivery and installation of supplies for traffic light and CCTV system.
observations.managementAction: City website is under repair.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the City Engineer and the City Planning and Development Coordinator to submit justification on their failure to comply with the provision of Item VI.A.3.e of the DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 68 Series of 2014 dated June 3, 2014 to issue a Warning Letter to the Contractor and require the submission of a Catch-up Plan to complete the works within the approved contract period.

We further recommend the CEO to submit SWA at target completion date and at date of actual completion. Furthermore, we recommend that the pre-audit section of the City Accounting Office impose liquidated damages, if circumstances warrant, based on the SWAs submitted by the CEO.
observations.managementAction: SWA has already been submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Circular 001-2015.

We further recommend that Management require the OIC-City Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institution (GFIs).
observations.managementAction: Management already requested approval from the BLGF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend once again that the Local School Board (LSB) and the OIC-City Treasurer, terminate the aforementioned time/savings deposits and maximize the utilization of the fund by mapping out more projects in line with the priority needs of public schools such as repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development in accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and other related rules.
observations.managementAction:
Please be informed that there are already identified projects by the Department of Education as approved by the Local School Board. In fact, various Purchase Requests have already been made and funded out of our SEF, approved by the local chief executive. Moreover, the said projects were already earmarked by the City Budget Office. We are waiting for the bidding of different projects and the Notice to Proceed given to the winning bidder. We  will  eventually  terminate  our  Special  Savings,  High  Yield  Savings and Time Deposits once the Current Account has been exhausted. It will only materialize if all the projects and plans of the city government will be fully implemented.

We ask for your kind understanding that we placed our SEF and Trust Fund to this account in order to augment the income of the City while waiting its overall implementation. This is part of our financial management and strategy to look for another way to generate income without hampering the city’s programs and projects to its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management impose the necessary liquidated damages specifically on the unjustified delay in the completion of the project be computed, and deducted from claim for final payment. If warranted and after due process, consider the contractor of the project for blacklisting.

We further recommend that implementing and procurement officers particularly the City Engineer, the OIC City Planning and Development Coordinator, and the BAC Chairperson, be required to submit their justification on their failure to monitor the prompt completion of the project, the lack of planning in the implementation of the project, failure to procure the furniture and appliances and airconditioning units on time, and their lack of initiative to recommend alternative courses of action to ensure the timely completion of the rehabilitation project.

Require the OIC-City General Services Officer to submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and Report on the Physical of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) of its inventory and PPE as of December 31, 2012 that would tally with the balances per books.

Require periodic physical count of inventory of supplies and PPE as prescribed in Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.

We advised the City Accountant’s Office to strictly adhere with the prescribed time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports.

Moreover, we advised management to review its standard operating procedures in the processing of claims, to recording of payments made and submission of documents from the Treasury Office to the Accounting Office and finally to the Auditor’s Office to address the delay in the submission of accounts.

We recommended that management appoint officials and personnel to conduct DRRM activities to assure that risk of lives and property be reduced in times of calamity and necessary and adequate disaster preparedness could be undertaken.  Further, we also recommended that CDRRMO be established.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the reports on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the City Waterworks Supervisor to assign personnel to repair all defective meters and henceforth, maintain them.  Also, strictly monitor unusual fluctuations in water meter consumptions that imply defect in the water meters to address such problems in a timely manner.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the defective meters were already replaced

observations.recommendation:
Adapt strategic means or method to intensify collection of delinquent taxes as mandated in Section 256 of RA 7160.

b. Instruct the City Accountant with the help of the City Treasurer and City Assessor to exert effort to work back on prior years’ record to review its accuracy and ensure correctness of journal entries.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers. Identified strategic means to intensify collection: Attended General Assembly in different barangays for RPT Tax Campaign. Conducted Public Auction Sale to the delinquent real property taxpayers

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board see to it that funds allocated for Special Education Fund should be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and the rules and regulations issued under the DECS (now DepED), DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01,01-A and 01-B to effectively attain the purpose for which the fund was created.  Assistance of this kind of expenditure may be appropriated out of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: A revised guideline on the use of the special education fund with reference to Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, series of 2017, has been issued by the Department of Education (DepEd), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), and Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), for the information and guidance of all concerned. Payment of school utilities is already allowed in the said guideline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management implement projects lined up for disaster preparedness and mainstream these programs in their activities to better prepare for and lessen the impact in case of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards/disasters.

We recommended that management close all accounts maintained at PVB and deposit the same to GFIs available in the locality in compliance to DOF Circular No. 001-2015.

We also recommended that management transfer all funds and cash balances with GFIs with less than CAMEL 3 rating to a compliant GFI after the lapse of the transition period in conformity with Section 6.1 of the DOF Circular No. 002.2015 dated 10 July 2015.

We recommended that management require the Project Engineer and the Team Leader of the Inspectorate Team to submit explanation on the Inspection Reports rendered which are inconsistent with the actual project status as shown in attached photos.

We further recommended that during final inspection of projects, this Office be informed so that simultaneous inspection could be done by the Inspectorate Team and COA personnel.

We recommended that management conduct an evaluation of the viability of the economic enterprises, consider alternative solutions and determine the best means of maximizing their operations to generate more revenue as well as minimize expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management made studies and activities to improve the performance of its economic enterprises.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC of the City Accounting and City General Services Offices coordinate and support the accounts recorded in the GL/TB, with SL of PPEs to establish the accuracy of recording of the assets in the financial statements and to give information on the location and end-user of the property.
observations.managementAction: Current acquisitions are recorded in subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee practice wide dissemination of bid opportunities, send invitation to independent observers, extend equal opportunity and issue bid documents to all qualified parties to dispel presumptions of rigged biddings and promote transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Management had done the required means in disseminating information for bids opportunities. However, due to the new documentary requirements of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, only few bidders qualified to bid for City infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended that management prioritize and consider filling-up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management hired personnel to fill-up some of the positions of the City Government Offices. Although the total 224 vital positions were not all filled-up during CY 2017, recruitment had been done to comply the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Require evaluation on the progress of project implementation and if warranted, rescind the contract with the contractor, without prejudice to other course of action and remedies open to it as provided in Section 68 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

To have quality roads, instruct the implementing office to have a closer supervision of project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Projects referred to in this observation were already completed in CY 2017. Constant evaluation of the progress of the projects is encouraged.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: We submitted these reports to the DILG. Henceforth, we will also furnish the COA with a copy.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Human Resource Management Office to re-evaluate the staffing composition of the City Government and the necessity of filling-up the vacant positions and hiring additional permanent personnel to render the function of regular employees and to boost the morale of those job order personnel that are qualified and highly competent of their positions;

Require the Budget Officer to review the appropriation for personal services and consider proposing in the budget the filling up of vacant positions and even of hiring of permanent employees if necessity and circumstances warrant.

We recommended that the City BAC Secretariat ensure the submission of contracts and POs and all supporting documents within five (5) days from the issuance or execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management always submit copies of contracts and POs and all supporting documents to the City Auditors Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City-HRMO to strictly observe CSC Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002, in hiring professional services under the Contract of Services, specifically the contract duration which shall not exceed six (6) months and the payment should be on a daily basis as provided under Section 1.a.2 of the said resolution.
observations.managementAction: Hired professionals under Contract of Service in accordance with RA 9184

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned barangay officials to liquidate all financial assistance received especially those granted for the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into account the required documentary requirements per COA Circular No. 2009-001 and refrain from granting additional financial assistance to barangays particularly those with substantial unliquidated balances.
observations.managementAction: Financial assistance on sports events was liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and that disbursement vouchers pertaining to payment of this transaction be supported with the following documents:

a. Duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets using the prescribed form which shall be secured before undertaking any travel using government vehicles. These should be serially numbered.

b. Requisition and Issue Vouchers

c. Daily Gas Issue Voucher

Further, require the Supply Officer or the OIC-City GSO to regularly prepare the following monthly reports to support the payment of fuel, oil and lubricants, to be submitted to the Audit Team on a periodic basis, to wit:

1. Summary of Requisition and Issue Vouchers
2. Monthly Report of Official Travels
3. Report of Fuel Consumption
observations.managementAction: Gathered documents and prepared the reports for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City’s Bid and Award Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: On-going consolidation of report by assigned personnel.

Submitted the list of all on-going projects and projects to be implemented This Year; Notify the COA Audit Team that signboards are already posted within ten (10) days after the award of infrastructure projects or before the program/activities started.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the City Mayor require the City Engineer’s Office to submit to COA Audit Team the suspension orders of the implementation of infrastructure projects totaling P32,363,450.21. Otherwise, require the OIC-City Accountant to impose liquidated damages to contractors in compliance with Section 68 of Republic Act 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the documents in support of the delayed accomplishment of the contractors of various infrastructure/rehabilitation projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the OIC City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of priority development projects, programs and activities during the year the funds are actually appropriated to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and desired outcomes from the programs/projects.

We recommended that Management discontinue to attach the picture and name of the mayor/any political personality on government programs, projects and activities (PPA) and adhere strictly to the aforecited provisions of COA Circular COA Circular 2013-004 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101.
observations.managementAction: Old supplies were consumed until supplies last.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management designate immediately official/employee to take charge of the reconciliation of billing statements to eliminate the surcharges.

We recommend that management designate immediately official/employee to take charge of the reconciliation of billing statements to eliminate the surcharges.
observations.managementAction: Only the current bills are paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate compliance of the full disclosure policy of the Department of the Interior and Local Government Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2010-083 dated August 31, 2010, as amended. Further, we recommend that management in coordination with the City Public Information Office, regularly update the postings in the designated bulletin boards in at least three (3) publicly accessible and conspicuous places in the City and also in the City’s website.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the concerned officers to post the required financial documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management prepare the List of Government Purchases/Contracts for Services and submit the same within 15 days after the end of each quarter to the BIR in compliance with the requirements of Presidential Memorandum Order No. 219. Likewise, furnish this Office of the same.
observations.managementAction: Submit the reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Budget Officer use the proper account classification in obligating transactions and the OIC City Accountant to record them in conformity with state accounting standards.
observations.managementAction: Obligate and record in accordance with what is authorized under the appropriation ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
Issue demand letters to all concerned parties to settle their outstanding cash advances immediately, otherwise, cause the withholding or suspension of salaries of officials and employees concerned for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445;

Request for authority to write-off unliquidated accounts for more than 10 years so that these amounts can be closed and free the books of accounts from misstated balances.  Refer to the Resident Auditor for guidelines in writing off these cash advances.
observations.managementAction: P25.1M confidential expenses liquidated but no credit advice from COA. The rest are advances by retired, deceased & separated employees.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Gowil Builders to immediately correct the defects and to adhere strictly to the provisions of the R-IRR of RA 9184;

Withhold final payment unless corrections of these defects are found effected.

Require the concerned city department officials to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations;

Prepare liquidation report of those projects already completed which were funded out of the fund under the account “Due from other NGAS” and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.

We will make adjusting entry this year.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC-City Treasurer to withhold the payment of any money due to Ms. Sonia G. Ingles and Mr. Lorell S. Ingles and take appropriate legal action to immediately recover the full amount.

Direct the OIC-City Treasurer to closely supervise the operations of the City Treasurer’s Office more particularly, in the collections, remittances, deposits and reporting of the said transactions.

observations.recommendation:
Require the OIC-Accountant to comply with the provisions stated in Section 30 of the NGAS Manual Volume III in recording and reporting fund transfers to other LGUs and Barangays.

Monitor the utilization of the funds released for project implementation by requiring the recipients to submit liquidation reports.

Require the barangays listed in Annex F to submit justification for non-liquidation/utilization of funds intended for development projects.

We recommended that management require the OIC-Accountant to observe proper recording of transactions in accordance with Section 32 of New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units Volume III, ensure that all fund releases to NGOs/POs be supported by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for their proper utilization, implementation and liquidation pursuant to the provisions stipulated under COA Circular 2007-001.

We recommended management to inform the persons liable/responsible of NDs/NS issued to prepare for settlement of the audit disallowances/suspensions. Those NDs within the appealable period that amounted to P10,095,564.30 are encouraged to appeal in accordance with the provisions of the RRSA.

For the NS, immediate settlement and or compliance to the suspension is encouraged and we recommend management to inform the persons responsible of the suspended transactions. Non-settlement and or compliance would cause issuance of an ND that would encompass a long process of settlement and even payment of disallowed amount,  or even worse, a case could be filed as further non-settlement could cause referral of the adverse audit finding to the Solicitor General and the Ombudsman.

We recommended that management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended that management shall take appropriate action to pinpoint responsibility on the spoiled food commodities and to  require refund for the cost thereof. Likewise, Management shall show proof that the unaccounted goods totaling P1,332,646.20 were properly distributed to the beneficiaries of the program, otherwise, this amount will not be allowed in audit.

We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to enforce the submission of the required liquidation documents for the financial assistance granted to the concerned barangays where the intended projects/activities have already been implemented in order to reflect the true and fair presentation of the account Due from LGUs. We also recommend that henceforth, the Accountant should monitor the submission by the barangays concerned of the liquidation report for the financial assistance released to them.
observations.managementAction: Follow-ups have been made to barangays considering that most of these transactions were before the time of the present barangay officials.

observations.recommendation:
Comply with the Guidelines in granting, utilization, accounting and auditing the funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 and the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by and between the LGU-Tagbilaran City and the NGO to avoid disallowance in audit.

(2) Submit explanation for the release of amount despite non-compliance of COA Circular 2007-001.

Closely monitor and inspect the project implementation and verify the financial records and reports on the progress of the projects submitted by the NGO.

(2) Submit explanation for the release of full amount despite that POW only amounts to P488,204.35

We recommended that Management demand financial report from the NGO for the progress of the project implementation in compliance of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Continuously monitor and inspect the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend recording of transactions to its proper account to fairly present the agency’s financial statements as of any given date.
observations.managementAction: Recording is based on appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Assessor to effect the general revision of real property assessments immediately and every three (3) years thereafter in order to increase real property tax income that could be spent for priority projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: Finalized the schedule of market values for lands, buildings and other structures. Formally requested assistance from the members of the Technical Working Group – Department of Finance, Engr. Dizon – Provincial Assessor of Pampanga, and Engr. Gilbas- Asst. Provincial Assessor of Sorsogon, to study and assist in coming up with a more realistic Schedule of Market Values.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Sangguniang Panlungsod enact an ordinance to stop and correct the effectivity of the Ordinance No. IIC-003 dated September 7, 2012 considering that it is legally applicable to the period CY 2013 only as provided in Article 282 (iv).
observations.managementAction: Had been taken for deliberation in the Sanggunian Panglungsod (SP). However, majority members of the SP are inclined not to enact an ordinance to correct the effectivity of the previous ordinance being questioned.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer provides the final computation of RPT/SET including penalties on unpaid real property for the periods CY 2005 to 2012 and 2014 to present and demand immediate payment of the same in order that the government may not be deprived of additional revenues to finance other developmental projects.

Further, submission of Gross Income Statement during these periods has to be demanded from the establishment for the computation and payment of business taxes.

Otherwise, the City Government may avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property and sale of real property by public auction or by judicial action.
observations.managementAction: As per AAPSI for CY 2015 dated August 25, 2016, billing statement was sent to ASC-ICM.  However, as per his letter dated March 22, 2017, the City Treasurer stated that they did not proceed in sending RPT billings to ASC-ICM due to some legal issues that had come up and until now not yet resolved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the CSWMO In-Charge to properly monitor the actual accomplishments of the Swampy Bulldozer Operator at the dumpsite and to submit explanation on the accomplishment made which are inconsistent with the actual services rendered.

We further recommended that a person will be assigned to monitor the work done by the Swampy Operator and actual accomplishment will be recorded thereon.

We also recommended that instead of renting a bulldozer, management appropriate amount for the purchase of a bulldozer for the Dumpsite.
We recommended that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to follow timelines indicated in the APP of the city government.
observations.managementAction: Monitor the timelines indicated in the APP by each concerned department as BAC only accept those covered by OBR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require each recipient official/employee to refund P65,000.00 and henceforth observe strictly what is allowed by law.
observations.managementAction: This is a Sangguniang Panglungsod discretion which involves legal questions. The disbursement was based on Ordinance No. 14-7 approving Supplemental Budget No. 5 series of 2014 which included the appropriation of the PEI of P70,000 for each official and employee. This ordinance likewise based on DBM Circular No. 2014-3 in relation to the Supreme Court decision in the case of Hon. RTC Judge Dadole, et.al vs. COA (G.R. No. 125350, December 3, 2002).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that two of the three CEO officials be relieved from their being members of the BAC/Secretariat/TWG to give these officials time to closely supervise and monitor infrastructure projects implementation.
observations.managementAction: Their knowledge and expertise are needed. There are projects delayed because of conflict of duties in their offices and in the BAC/Secretariat/TWG.

observations.recommendation:
Create an inventory committee as prescribed in Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386.

Require the committee to conduct inventory and submit inventory report to the local chief executive copy furnished the Accountant Office and the Commission On Audit.

Based on the inventory report, require the Accounting Office to reconcile in their record and prepare adjustment when applicable.
observations.managementAction: Disclosed in Notes to FS. Created the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against Sonia G. Ingles, Cristina N. Corloncito and all other collecting officers found to have falsified official receipts and withheld collections for their personal use as listed in the presentation of this finding and those of CY 2012 AAR.
observations.managementAction: The present administration do not have the possession of the documents which are the subject of the audit observation.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC City Accountant to include in their accounting system the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledger of Collecting Officers;

Direct the OIC City Accountant, with the cooperation with the OIC City Treasurer, to reconcile the records of the Accounting with that of the Collectors at least once every quarter.
observations.managementAction: Inapplicable to Accounting. No SL for collectors in the current system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that collection data and/or the subsidiary ledger to be maintained by the accounting department for the collectors would include, aside from the collection date (date of OR) and amount, the RCR number and the date remittances were made by the collectors.
observations.managementAction: There is no SL for collectors; no feature for this item even in the e-TRACs

observations.recommendation:
Require the signatories of the documents enumerated earlier and/or the contractors or suppliers to submit justification/explanation on the deficiencies noted.

Require the OIC-City Accountant  gather the documents, explanations as well as other reports in compliance to the suggested actions for each deficiency noted in the transactions.

Subsequent transactions be supported with valid and proper documents and that deficiencies noted in this finding would not exist on those transactions.

We recommended that management require the Offices of the General Services and the City Accountant to book up the appraised value of the 467 lots to reflect the actual amounts of the Land, Government Equity or Public Infrastructure accounts, including that of the remaining 45 lots as soon as the appraisal shall have been completed, and the Office of the General Services to keep a complete record of the same, including the real property card.
observations.managementAction: We are requesting COA to please give us the documents for us to effect the entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management request approval from the Bureau of
Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant
with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Circular 001-2015.
We further recommend that Management require the OIC-City Treasurer to
transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing
bank accounts with authorized government financial institution (GFIs).

We recommend once again that the Local School Board (LSB) and the OIC-
City Treasurer, terminate the aforementioned time/savings deposits and maximize
the utilization of the fund by mapping out more projects in line with the priority
needs of public schools such as repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment,
educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development in
accordance with Section 272 of RA 7160 and other related rules.

We recommend that the OIC-City Treasurer requires all check
issuance/fund/bank account be recorded in the Check Register.

We recommend that management require the ICO-City Treasurer and OIC-
City Accountant to reconcile the account balances per Cashbook and per General
Ledger. Identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in
the affected books to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.

We recommend that the Accountable Officer be required to maintain
cashbook to record his transactions and have it be made personally by him. The
cashbook shall be kept in the office of the accountable officer and placed inside the
safe or cabinet when not in use.

Refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash
advances given to the special disbursing officer is first settled or a proper
accounting thereof has been made.

We recommend that since these Accountable Officers (AO) were not
actually doing the disbursing functions on cash advances drawn under their names,
no further cash advances should be granted to these AOs to properly safeguard
government funds.

We recommend that management provide adequate safety vault for
safekeeping the cash/money of the Petty Cash Custodian/Special Disbursing
Officers.

We recommend that:
a. The City Accountant demand the immediate liquidation of fund transfer/
financial assistance granted to various barangays for CY 2016 amounting
to ₱4,712,272.39 by setting specific timeliness for the liquidation
considering that the terms of the agreement had already been violated.
b. The City Mayor assign official or personnel to be responsible in
monitoring as well as validation of actual accomplishments vis-vis
purpose for which the fund transfer was granted.

We recommend that management officially designate a PPE Inventory
Committee and immediately schedule the conduct of a physical count of all assets to
ensure a fair presentation of the PPE and Inventory accounts in the financial
statements. Once the RPCPPE and RPCI are made, we recommend that the same
be reconciled with the stock/property cards, ledger cards and with the control
accounts.

We recommend that management require the members of the Disposal
Committee to evaluate the status of unserviceable properties and facilitate its
immediate disposal by fast tracking the preparation of documents needed therefor
in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommend that management create an ad hoc team that will
concentrate on the updating of property records and reconciliation of the balances
with the City Accounting Office.

We recommend that management direct the officials concerned to fast-track
the provision of the much-needed assistance in order to accomplish the purposes for
which the funds were granted and deliver immediate and optimum results to target
beneficiaries of the Emergency Shelter Assistance.

We recommend that Management:
a. Stop the payment of Transportation Allowance to those Local Officials and
Employees who are assigned or who use government motor transportation
for the actual performance of their duties and responsibilities as prescribed
in the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to avoid
disallowance in the future.
b. Direct the concerned officials to refund the Transportation Allowance (TA)
claimed during the period wherein they used the motor vehicles.

We recommend that the officials listed earlier submit explanation for not
having their attendance be registered in the biometric machine despite its
availability at the agency’s premises. Further, justification from the City Human
Resource Officer is required for her failure to have the CSC ruling on attendance
registry be enforced. Henceforth, CSC ruling on attendance registry have to be
adhered to by all appointed permanent/co-terminus city government officials.

We recommend that the City Human Resource and Management Officer be
required to submit the aforementioned requirements and provide legal basis for
hiring the 8 consultants paid higher rates.

We recommend that management evaluate and consider
implementation/increasing the daily wage rates of job order personnel.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Job Order personnel
prepare and submit their actual accomplishment reports to support their services
rendered. We recommend also that the management avoid disbursement of funds
with incomplete documentation.

We recommend that Management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by
filling up the mandatory positions in keeping with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of
RA 10121, to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk
reduction and management.

We recommend that management:
a) Require the City Accounting Office to immediately submit the 1,104 DVs
amounting to ₱15,051,467.41 together with all the supporting documents to
the COA for verification and to minimize suspension and/or outright
disallowances;
b) Instruct the City Treasurer’s Office to communicate the availability of
checks to Payees to facilitate the timely payment of government
transactions; and
c) Undertake measures to facilitate the timely and complete submission of the
DVs within the first ten days of the ensuing month as required under
Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit explanation on
the implementation of projects not in accordance with the approved APP.
Henceforth, submit APP to the office of the City COA.

We recommend that Management submit an explanation for indicating
effectivity date in the NTP that is more than seven (7) days from its issuance which
is not in accordance with Section 37.4.1of the R-IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Head, City BAC Secretariat submit copy of the
posting of the NTP in PhilGEPS and the City website fifteen (15) days from its
issuance.

We recommend management to require the City Accountant to deduct from
the payment to the supplier the liquidated damages to be imposed on late delivery
and installation of supplies for traffic light and CCTV system.

We recommend Management to terminate the project pursuant to Item 3.2,
Annex D, R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and, if warranted and after due process, consider
the contractor for blacklisting.

We recommend that management require the City Engineer and the City
Planning and Development Coordinator to submit justification on their failure to
comply with the provision of Item VI.A.3.e of the DILG Memorandum Circular
(MC) No. 68 Series of 2014 dated June 3, 2014 to issue a Warning Letter to the
Contractor and require the submission of a Catch-up Plan to complete the works
within the approved contract period.
We further recommend the CEO to submit SWA at target completion date
and at date of actual completion. Furthermore, we recommend that the pre-audit
section of the City Accounting Office impose liquidated damages, if circumstances
warrant, based on the SWAs submitted by the CEO.

Advice the City Treasurer to demand official receipts printed by the National
Printing Office upon release of the membership fees, annual dues and registration
fees to the Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines (VMLP) and the Philippine
Councilors League Incorporated (PCL). At no instance shall old receipts be honored
to acknowledge payments.

We recommend that management:
a.) Confer with the National Solid Waste Commission regarding the approval of
the submitted 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan so that such plan could
be implemented.
b.) Give primary attention in the preparation and implementation of projects
and activities for safe and sanitary management of solid waste including the
conduct of information dissemination that will educate the constituents of the
appropriate practices in the handling of wastes, sorting, segregation,
composting, or recycling and appropriate sufficient funds for the said
purpose.
c.) Strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under
RA No. 9003.

We recommend that Management impose the necessary liquidated damages
specifically on the unjustified delay in the completion of the project be computed,
and deducted from claim for final payment. If warranted and after due process,
consider the contractor of the project for blacklisting.

We further recommend that implementing and procurement officers
particularly the City Engineer, the OIC City Planning and Development
Coordinator, and the BAC Chairperson, be required to submit their justification on
their failure to monitor the prompt completion of the project, the lack of planning in
the implementation of the project, failure to procure the furniture and appliances
and airconditioning units on time, and their lack of initiative to recommend
alternative courses of action to ensure the timely completion of the rehabilitation
project.

We recommend that the OIC-City Planning and Development Coordinator,
City Engineer and City Budget Officer be required to facilitate the implementation
of these projects and be made to explain on its non-implementation at timelines set
for its implementation.

We recommend that the City Government strictly observe the provisions set
forth under Section 5.2 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01
dated April 5, 2004, including the preparation and implementation of the GAD
plans, budget and submit accomplishment report as required in the aforecited
provision.
